corrosim — multiscale corrosion-inhibitor report

Substrate Fe(110)  |  Medium 1 M HCl  |  DFT level B3LYP/6-311++G(d,p) (ddCOSMO:water)  |  Generated 2026-06-29 15:19

Flavonoids modelled here (kaempferol, quercetin, isorhamnetin) are documented major constituents of the extract, simulated as representatives — not a verified profile of a specific sample. Confirm composition by LC-MS/GC-MS before publication.

quercetin4.0822.0410.490.178-15.973.650.995
isorhamnetin4.0992.0490.4880.21-16.743.750.373
kaempferol4.1462.0730.4820.169-16.553.35-1.368

Composite score z-scores a smaller gap, lower hardness and higher softness (higher = stronger predicted reactivity). Eads (Stage 2 Monte Carlo) and the Fe–O distance (Stage 3 MD) are shown alongside for the adsorption picture.

Stage 1  DFT electronic descriptors

Stage 1b  Local reactivity (Fukui)

Condensed Fukui / dual-descriptor maps locate the donor and acceptor atoms. The strongest electron-donating oxygens (highest f⁻) are the metal-coordinating sites:

  • kaempferol: O3 (f⁻=0.116), O12 (f⁻=0.055), O19 (f⁻=0.053)
  • quercetin: O3 (f⁻=0.090), O9 (f⁻=0.088), O11 (f⁻=0.040)
  • isorhamnetin: O9 (f⁻=0.094), O3 (f⁻=0.093), O11 (f⁻=0.043)

Stage 2  Monte Carlo adsorption

Metropolis/annealing pose search on the metal surface. Flavonoids settle flat/parallel; Eads ≈ −16 kJ/mol indicates physisorption.

Stage 3  Brownian MD — Fe–O RDF

Rigid-body Brownian dynamics from the best MC pose; the first Fe–O radial-distribution peak (~3.5 Å) sets the adsorption distance.

Method & caveats

Descriptors from frontier-orbital energies (Koopmans). DFT level: B3LYP/6-311++G(d,p) (ddCOSMO:water). ΔN uses the metal work function with η(metal)=0. Monte Carlo / Brownian MD use a classical van-der-Waals adsorption model — a screening surrogate, not a substitute for periodic DFT or electrochemical validation. The composite ranking is a heuristic.
